name: Test resume
Resume_Shrey (1)
text:
SHREY BISHNOI
| shreybishnoi.dev@gmail.com | LinkedIn | GitHub
SUMMAR Y
Software Engineer and MS Computer Science student with 2+ years of experience building and maintaining production backend and full-stack
systems. Strong foundation in object-oriented design, data structures, RESTful APIs, and relational databases, with a focus on performance,
reliability, and clean system design. Comfortable owning features end-to-end within modern SDLC and Agile environments.
EDUCA TION
Arizona State University (GPA: 4.11/4.0) Aug 2025 – May 2027
Master of Science, Computer Science Tempe, AZ, USA
Maharaja Agrasen Institute of T echnology (GP A: 3.7/4.0) Aug 2019 – Jul 2023
Bachelor of Science, Computer Science and Engineering New Delhi, India
SKILLS
Languages: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Java, SQL
Backend & APIs: RESTful services, OOP design, data modeling, ETL pipelines, performance optimization
Fr o nt e n d :React, Vue.js-style frameworks, HTML, CSS
Databases & Systems: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Kafka, RocksDB, Neo4j
T ools & Platforms: Git, Docker, Kubernetes (Minikube), A WS (EC2, RDS), Linux, CI/CD
Practices: Agile SDLC, code reviews, testing, documentation, analytical problem-solving
EXPERIENCE
Software Engineer | ZS Associates Sep 2023 – Jun 2025
• Owned and maintained 5+ production backend services using Python, Node.js, and PostgreSQL , supporting analytics workﬂows
for 3+ product and data teams and enabling reliable data delivery at scale.
• Optimized data-intensive REST APIs by tuning SQL queries, adding indexes, and refactoring service logic, reducing report latency by
40% and improving overall system responsiveness.
• Automated ETL pipelines in Python and SQL and built 3+ internal React dashboards for monitoring system health, data freshness,
and KPIs, cutting manual analyst e !ort by 40% and improving operational visibility.
Software Engineer Intern | Dreadnought School Jun 2022 – Aug 2022
• Implemented backend features using strong OOP principles and data structures within a client–server architecture, contributing to
10+ feature modules with clean, maintainable service design.
• Designed and consumed HTTP/REST APIs , gaining hands-on experience with request lifecycles, validation, and error handling while
working with PostgreSQL/MySQL across multiple relational schemas .
• Developed responsive UI components using modern JavaScript frameworks (Vue.js-style patterns) and collaborated using Git in a 5+
member team , gaining exposure to CI/CD pipelines, cloud deployments, and Agile workﬂows .
Full-Stack Developer Intern | Andwemet Pvt Ltd Jun 2021 – Oct 2021
• Built a customer-facing onboarding system using the MERN stack , designing and implementing frontend user ﬂows while developing
backend APIs to handle user data, validation, and onboarding logic for 100+ active users .
• Improved onboarding e ” ciency through iterative UI reﬁnements and backend performance improvements, reducing onboarding time by
35% and increasing feature adoption by 50% based on user interaction patterns.
Teaching Assistant | Coding Ninjas Jun 2022 – Sep 2022
• Spearheaded mentorship program for 100+ students in C++ data structures and algorithms ,o !ering guidance on core concepts and
reducing student debugging time by 15% through personalized code reviews.
• Reviewed 200+ code submissions , identiﬁed logical errors and edge cases, and explained trade-o!sa n do p t i m i z a t i o n sclearly, helping
students improve correctness, performance, and coding discipline.
PROJECTS
Real-Time Graph Analytics System | Kafka, RocksDB, Neo4j, Docker, Kubernetes
• Designed a distributed analytics system ingesting high-throughput event streams via Kafka, enabling scalable, fault-tolerant
real-time data processing across multiple containerized services .
• Managed application state using RocksDB and executed graph algorithms with Neo4j, deploying services using Docker and Kubernetes
to achieve 2→ throughput under concurrent workloads.
Gym Occupancy Tracker | Next.js, Supabase, PostgreSQL, REST APIs
• Engineered a real-time gym occupancy platform used by live users to estimate crowd levels throughout the day, designing a privacy-
ﬁrst data model with anonymous device hashes to support check-ins, automatic check-outs, and user surveys.
• Implemented a decay-based aggregation algorithm to compute stable occupancy percentages from raw event data, computing all metrics
on-demand (no cron jobs) while handling timezone-aware logic and enforcing rate-limiting to prevent abuse.
